title: What Money Story Do I Want to Pass Forward?
subtitle: Journal prompts for examining the financial patterns you inherited and the ones you are building now
excerpt: Five journal prompts for examining generational money patterns. The prompts move from what you absorbed about money growing up to what you want the people around you to absorb from watching you. The aim is specificity, not a grand rewrite.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 60 reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to examine where your money beliefs came from and what pattern you are currently passing on to the people around you.

content.text: Five journal prompts for examining what you absorbed about money growing up and what you are now building in its place. These prompts don't ask for a diagnosis of your childhood — they ask for specific observations and specific intentions. Set twenty minutes and write without stopping.
New to journalling as practice? Read
What did money mean in the house I grew up in?
Not the amount of it. The feeling around it. Write the specific words, silences, or arguments you remember.
How would it feel if everyone connected to me benefited from my abundance?
Write the feeling first. Then write where there is hesitation or guilt in that picture.
What money pattern do I still carry that I didn't choose?
Name one specific behaviour or belief. 'Checking the balance seventeen times a day.' 'Never spending on myself.' 'Giving before I have enough.' Be specific.
What new story can I create that honours my own success without guilt?
Write the story in one paragraph. Present tense. 'In my family, money means...' Write where it ends now, not where it started.
Who in my family am I grateful for today, and how has abundance already touched their life?
Name one person. Write one specific thing abundance has given them — small or large.
Adapted from the Day 60 reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
